paper_title: Numerical Methods for Single-Photon Ionization of H2+ in XUV Fields

methods.method_name: Time-Dependent Perturbation Theory for Transition Amplitude
methods.description: This method uses time-dependent perturbation theory to express the transition amplitude for the single-photon ionization of H2+ in XUV fields. It involves calculating the transition amplitude and expectation value of the photoelectron momentum.

steps.description: Express the transition amplitude using time-dependent perturbation theory.
steps.searchable_summary: apply time-dependent perturbation theory to express transition amplitude

steps.description: Calculate the expectation value of the photoelectron momentum pz.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate expectation value of photoelectron momentum pz

methods.method_name: Molecular Orbital Construction for Diatomic Molecules
methods.description: This method constructs molecular orbitals for diatomic molecules like H2+, N2, and O2 using atomic states and Gaussian type orbital (GTO) basis sets. It involves calculating molecular orbitals and their coefficients and exponents.

steps.description: Construct molecular orbitals for H2+ by combining two atomic states.
steps.searchable_summary: combine atomic states to construct molecular orbitals for H2+

steps.description: Calculate molecular orbitals for N2 and O2 using MOLPRO with GTO basis cc-pVTZ.
steps.searchable_summary: use MOLPRO to calculate molecular orbitals for N2 and O2 with GTO basis
outputs: molecular orbitals for N2 and O2
